content: Why you should go to GuangZhou if you are a foodie When we were living in Hong Kong, we went on a weekend getaway to GuangZhou, and to be honest I kind of regret not doing it more often. If you are a foodie, this place is like heaven on earth. I have been to many cities and provinces in China, but this city has the best food by far. And the funniest thing is we discovered a hidden-gem by accident. We had a free weekend and didn’t really know where to go. We already traveled to ShenZhen quite often, so we decided to explore a new city. My girlfriend really likes coconut hotpot, and she heard there are very good ones in GuangZhou. Even though I don’t like it that much, I still decided to tell her “yes let’s do it” and thank god I said it, because we discovered one street with some of the best food in China. We took a train from Hong Kong train station to GuangZhou (only 40 minutes!!!) After we arrived in the city, we decided to take a taxi to the hotpot place. On the way, suddenly the cab slowed down and we stopped moving :( We looked outside and the street was packed with people and food stalls. Everyone was sitting on the sidewalks and eating so many different kinds of food. We were so hungry because we didn’t eat the entire day. So we just asked the taxi driver to leave us there, and little did we know we discovered the BEST street food street! We walked around and entered so many different stores and shops, and tried so many kinds of food. Food we tried: fried pigeon( 炸乳鸽) - the best pigeon I had in my life black chicken soup (乌鸡药材汤) coconut chicken soup (椰子鸡汤)- inside the coconut itself!! black sesame fudge (芝麻糊) premium durian and much more that I already forgot The food was so good, so instead of going for a day trip we decided to stay over in GuangZhou. That way we had a chance to fill our belly with more amazing food we hadn’t managed to eat that day. I posted pictures here of the food. I’m sorry if the quality is not the best. The food was too good so we finished everything before having a chance to take a picture. (I left a picture of me playing ping pong with locals as an apology.) And I know you might think “Why does this laowai know about Chinese food?” Let me tell you this: I traveled to China 14 times in the past year, all the way from HaiNan to Beijing, so if I see good food I know to stop and appreciate it. If you still don’t believe me, take a look at the comments below. Secret street location: 司马路/西华路路口 If you have no plans this coming weekend, you must go there and give it a try! I really encourage you to go tomorrow (or on your next day off) and let me know how it goes. I hope I convinced you. Any other places you want to hear my opinion about? Do you think there are places with better food? (please don’t say Beijing - I didn’t really like the food there - but that is another story already)
title: Why you should go to GuangZhou if you are foodie

content: Why I left Hong Kong: I lived for about 1 year in Hong Kong. This was an amazing yet very chaotic experience. I met my girlfriend there while traveling and decided to stay with her (she lived there for about 3 years). Hong Kong was really too much for us. Don’t get me wrong. I think Hong Kong is an amazing travel destination and you should definitely go there. The scenery is amazing and the food is so good. It’s just not a place to live and build a life in. Hong Kong people - at least most of them - are rude, not really nice, and too self-centered. Every time I would walk on a crowded street or step into a crowded metro, I felt like I was fighting with everyone for space and to be seen. Our space in Hong Kong was tiny! (less than 40 sqm). It is really hard to live life in this small “shoe box”. After some time, I felt Hong Kong got the best of me and made me become a person I don’t want to be. I started to be less nice to people around me, less patient, and got stressed about small things easily. It was really too hard, so we decided to leave. We just moved to Singapore and so far the experience is very different. The pathways are wide enough so you don’t need to push anyone (hahahah). Our house is much bigger and nicer, there is a lot of greenery around us, and we are just around the corner from a river and a park, so we don’t need to go on a hike to escape the city (like HK). Most importantly, we feel like the work-life balance, while not as good as Europe, is much better and healthier than Hong Kong. I posted pictures here of us having fun in Hong Kong. I know I spread some hate about Hong Kong here, but we really had some great times and memories there. This place will always have a warm place in my heart. Hopefully one day I can come back as a tourist, go to the horse racing, and experience the big city life. What is your experience with Hong Kong? I want to know if people have a similar experience to what we had. And what do you think about Singapore? I would love to hear from people who have already lived here for a long time and how it compares to Hong Kong. What do you prefer? Hong Kong Singapore neither…
title: Why I left Hong Kong

content: Why you have to go see horse racing while you are in Hong Kong When I was living in Hong Kong, I had a chance to experience horse racing in Happy Valley, and to be honest, that was one of my best memories from the city. Before I start, don’t be like “Yeah I live in Hong Kong, I will go check it out one day…” We both know you won’t. Read this and hopefully it will convince you. Horse racing in Happy Valley happens every Wednesday! (except during summer). The races actually happen during evening-night time, making it one of the best attractions to do after a hard day of work. And what is my favorite thing about it? You can drink beer and bet! Betting actually starts from a very small amount (20 HKD), so usually we would set a small budget (200 HKD) and slowly spend it throughout the night. I don’t really encourage betting - we were doing it for the experience hahahah (and I never won). What I really loved though was the atmosphere there. You see the older generation of Hong Kong standing around with newspapers, studying numbers, doing calculations, discussing horses like their life depends on it. It makes you realize how deeply horse racing is connected to the culture of the city. The environment becomes really thrilling once the race is about to start. People slowly gather near the track. Everyone is waiting. Then suddenly the horses fly past you at full speed. You really start to admire those animals. That was probably the craziest part for me. You can stand right next to the track and literally feel the wind from the horses when they pass by. The ground shakes a little, people start screaming, and for a few seconds the entire place feels electric. I posted pictures here of us having the time of our lives at the racing track. Race night is really one of my favorite nights in Hong Kong. For drinking, there are bars inside where you can grab beer. Or “DON’T TELL ANYONE” just buy beers from 7-Eleven hide them in your work bag. There are multiple races throughout the night so the flow is basically: Bet → Race → Break → Repeat. The entrance uses the Octopus card actually (makes you realize how deeply it’s baked into Hong Kong culture). You can come anytime and leave anytime during the event. address: 跑馬地馬場 How to get there: You can take the old tram right to Happy Valley, then it’s a short 5-minute walk to the main entrance. For more information you can look at the Happy Valley official site. What was your experience going to horse racing? What do you think about the event? Did you manage to win anything (we only lose so far! hahah). Are there any other activities in Hong Kong that I missed out on / you want me to cover?
title: Why you have to go see horse racing while you are in Hong Kong

content: I’ve stayed for 3 weeks in the begginging of 2024 in this magical island in the south of Thailand. When I was there i rented my own scooter and scoot around. it was basically a ‘beach day everyday’ for me! In my time there i discovered a couple of nice coast lines and beaches that i will hightly recommend. I wrote a short review for each beach so lets get started
title: Best Beaches in Ko pha-ngan

content: Me and my girlfriend spent about 4 days in Okinawa as a quick getaway trip. During that time, I decided to book a flight to this super tiny island called Kumejima (久米島町) - just a 30-minute flight (or a 4-hour boat ride) from the main island. We only had 30 hours there, but honestly, it was the best decision we made on this whole trip! Our tiny 40-seater airplane We landed in Kumejima pretty early, around 11AM, and had a few hours to kill before hotel check-in - and to be honest, we really didn’t know what to expect. We brought our snorkel masks from home and just hoped for the best. According to the internet, there are only two bus lines on the island… We only saw one — and it comes every two hours. There were also just two taxis waiting at the airport. Hahaha. When we arrived, we thought: maybe we should try to hitchhike? I’ve done it a few times before (like here) and my girlfriend really wanted to give it a try. And let me tell you - it was a great success. In just 30 hours, we managed to hitchhike with 6 different people! First hitchhike - with a marine biologist who was on our flight
title: 30 Hours in Kumejima - the Best Island in Japan

content: Snorkeling in Paradise We only had 30 hours on this island, so we ended up going to just 3–4 beaches! The first beach we went to was Eef Beach. This place seriously looked like paradise — 50 shades of blue in the water, pretty clean visibility, and an amazing view all the way to the horizon. It was only a 2-minute walk from our hotel, so it made the perfect first stop before we could check in. We literally hid our bags and changed into swimsuits in the middle of the road. The beach itself was great. There’s even a Family Mart within walking distance, so we grabbed some snacks and beers. We tried snorkeling there, but because it was a windy day, the water near the shore got pretty sandy — not the greatest experience. After talking to some locals, we realized this is actually one of the least recommended places to snorkel in Kume… We still saw a bunch of fish, but it was nothing compared to what came next. Since check-in time still hadn’t arrived, I was like, “Let’s just walk a bit and maybe hitchhike our way to the east side of the island.” There are two tiny islands connected to the main one by a bridge, and I thought they might be a cool place to snorkel. Little did I know we’d end up hitchhiking with this super friendly and kind local named Kairi (you’ll hear more about him later). He picked us up, showed us around a bit, and then drove us all the way to this spot at the edge of the second island. He told us this was the perfect time to see sea turtles. We were so excited. Even though low tide was starting to kick in, we grabbed our gear and ran straight into the water. The water was super shallow - maybe 1 meter deep at most. We saw three white murenas swimming around, switching their little “camping spots” and hunting small fish. Then, just as the sun hit the water at the right angle, we started spotting them - sea turtles! So many of them, just casually swimming in the shallow water, munching on sea grass. We spent the next two hours walking around in the water, trying to get close to the turtles without scaring them off. But every time they noticed us, they zoomed away - I had no idea turtles could swim that fast. The beach across the road from our hotel After that, we were completely dead. We hitchhiked back with a Japanese guy who didn’t know a single word of English, but he somehow dropped us off exactly at our hotel. Called it a day. Well… kind of. Later that night we woke up starving - but turns out almost everything on the island closes ridiculously early. I’m talking 7PM and you’re out of luck. Thank god for Family Mart again. We grabbed whatever snacks we could find, and then remembered: Kairi, the guy who gave us a ride earlier, had told us he owns one of the only bars in Kume, so we decided to check it out. We headed over and ended up spending the night hanging out with him and his local basketball team - just drinking, chatting, and laughing through a mix of broken Japanese and random Google Translates. Our Kume experience wouldn’t have been the same without him! Tip Most places in Kumejima close super early — like, 7PM early. Always have backup snacks, or make a friend who owns a bar.
title: 30 Hours in Kumejima - the Best Island in Japan

content: Kumejima Adventure Continue: The Next Day We set the goal already at the beginning of the day: “Today we will swim with the turtles.” Let me tell you right away - we failed our mission. Again. But we saw something way crazier! Kairi told us you can sometimes spot turtles on the southeast part of the second island, so we decided to hitchhike again. This time, I think we made one of my dreams come true - we hitchhiked in the back of a truck! One of those mini Japanese trucks you only see in rural Japan or in the movies. Not the most comfortable ride, but honestly… it was amazing. The wind in your face, ocean views all around - we couldn’t ask for a better experience. Amazing experience The driver dropped us off at the abandoned Kumejima Museum (guess we were traveling in the off-season), and we started walking east along the coastline. We tried to jump into the water a couple of times, but the waves were too strong - felt unsafe - so we decided to just keep walking all the way to our favorite turtle-spotting area. At the eastern edge of the island, you can literally see the water current rushing between the second and third islands. For safety, we decided to snorkel close to shore. We jumped into the water, and not even 5 meters from the shore we saw this sudden drop in the ocean floor - and there it was: a beautiful coral reef. So many kinds of fish - cleaning fish, batfish, parrotfish, and others I have no idea how to name. We hung around the reef for a while, and every time the current pushed us away, we swam back. Not the safest thing we’ve ever done… but also not the worst :) We kept going, trying to spot turtles again - still no luck. We could see their heads popping above the water, but every time we tried to swim near them, they sped off. Still, the water was amazing, and the spot was peaceful. Once the low tide started again, we decided to swim back toward the reef - our starting point. And then we saw it. A sea snake. One of the most venomous animals on this planet. I completely panicked (for no legit reason) and started pushing both me and my girlfriend back to shore. She said she’s never seen me swim that fast in her life. I really don’t know what happened to me - I’m usually super calm. Maybe it was the current, maybe it was the long snorkel session. Maybe just my brain bugging out. Still - it was an amazing experience. Once we were back on shore, I even jumped back in one last time to explore the reef a bit more. I wish we had more time to fully explore it… but we had to head back to the airport for our flight back to the Okinawa main island. The coastlines between two of the small islands in Kume
title: 30 Hours in Kumejima - the Best Island in Japan

content: Final Thoughts from Kume We honestly loved every second of being in Kume. Our 1.5 days were definitely not enough - I wish we had at least 2 more days to explore the island properly. Maybe even go SUP and Snorkeling with Kairi like we talked about. This island just felt different. We met some of the nicest people we’ve ever met in Japan, and the vibe overall was super welcoming and laid-back. Great weather, and honestly - one of the nicest beaches I’ve ever been to in my life. If you’re ever in Okinawa and wondering if it’s worth the detour - it is. The island isn’t too touristic, so you can have most of the beaches to yourself. Definitely take advantage of that. 😉
title: 30 Hours in Kumejima - the Best Island in Japan

content: Our Hidden Gems list 🍦 Kumejimaru Cafe (くめじまーるカフェ) - Salted ice cream in a giant cone. must try! 🏪 FamilyMart - The place that saved our lives many times – be aware: closes at 11PM. 🍜 Namiji resturant - Great place to try local Kume meal. 🍻 Bar Cheers - The Best and only bar in Kume! (shout out to our friend @Kairi 🏖️ Beaches: 🌊 Eef Beach - Amazing views and the best place to bath around. 🐢 Our sea turtles spot - Be aware of high/low tide and check the timetable. 🪸 The crazy coral reef we found - You can go there by foot from the sea turtles place or by just walking around the island (like we did). Full beach guide coming soon: Best Beaches in Kumejima Where we stayed We stayed in: Bears Stay Kumejima Eef Beach Very close to the Family Mart + Local resturant & bar The rooms where very big No reception. All the check-in happend in front of a machine, so you cannot check in early The cleaning lady was very nice and she gave us extra towels for the beach!
title: 30 Hours in Kumejima - the Best Island in Japan
